betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6Caesium-137§f

§aExtremely radioactive fission product — ~9% of short-lived nuclear waste!§f

§bCaesium-137§f (Cs-137) is an extremely radioactive caesium isotope and a common nuclear fission product (§e20 RAD/s§f). It is naturally pyrophoric.

§6Production§f:
§b• Short-Lived Nuclear Waste§f — Cs-137 makes up ~9% of short-lived waste on average
§b• MOX Fuel Cycles§f — reprocessing MOX fuel in the research reactor produces Cs-137
§b• Highly Enriched Pu-239 Fuel§f — research reactor fuel cycle yields Cs-137

§6Uses§f:
§b• Currently none§f — but has potential applications in §esalted nuclear weapons§f

§eTip§f: Cs-137 makes up ~9% of short-lived waste — you'll get significant quantities during PUREX reprocessing. Store it for future salted nuke recipes!
